title: Chefland Squads and the Army Chief

content.problemStatement:
The Chefland Army has NNN soldiers, standing in a row and numbered 111 through NNN from left to right. Their heights from left to right are A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.

A squad consists of one or more consecutive soldiers, i.e. soldiers LLL through RRR (inclusive) for some LLL and RRR such that 1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N. The strength of a squad is defined in the following way: for each soldier in this squad, find the number of soldiers in the squad with strictly greater heights that are standing to the left of this soldier, and sum up these numbers.

The Army Chief decided to write down the strengths of all possible squads, but soon, he realised that it is a heavy task. Therefore, he only wants to know the median of the strengths of all possible squads.

Note: The median of a sequence is the value that is in the middle when the sequence is sorted. If the length of the sequence is even, there are two values in the middle and the median is the smaller of these values (or either when they are equal).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the median of the strengths of all squads.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is only one possible squad and its strength is 000.
Example case 3: There are 101010 possible squads, with strengths (0,0,0,0,1,1,1,3,3,6)(0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 3, 3, 6)(0,0,0,0,1,1,1,3,3,6).
